Youth Education & Mental Wellness

Every child
deserves to thrive,
not just survive.

The N Johnson Foundation removes educational and financial barriers for children and families in Belize and the United States — so every student can show up prepared, supported, and ready to grow.

Our Purpose

Education & healing
go hand in hand.

The N. Johnson Foundation is a community-driven nonprofit built on the belief that a child cannot learn when they are hungry, afraid, or unseen. We work to ease financial burdens on families, promote emotional wellness, and make sure every student walks into school prepared — with supplies in hand and support in their corner.

Our work spans Belize and the United States, rooted in culturally respectful, community-first solutions that address both practical needs and long-term healing.

Mental Wellness Educational Equity Self-Love Healthy Boundaries Youth Empowerment Community First Emotional Intelligence
What We Do

Programs rooted in
real community needs.

Student Scholarships
Financial assistance ensuring students continue their education without interruption. We directly fund tuition, fees, and materials so families don't have to choose between bills and school.
Learn more →
School Supply Drives
Backpacks, uniforms, books, and essentials distributed directly to students in Belize and the U.S.
Learn more →
Mental Wellness Workshops
Upcoming sessions for students and families on emotional resilience, self-love, and healthy boundaries.
Coming soon →
School Donations
Classroom resources tailored directly to what teachers and students actually need to succeed.
Learn more →
Peer Mentorship
Trained peer leaders paired with younger students — creating safe, judgment-free spaces for open conversation, guidance, and emotional support.
Learn more →
The Book Initiative
Proceeds from Nicole Johnson's published novels — stories of resilience and healing — fund the Foundation's programs directly.
Shop on Amazon →
Our Impact to Date

Small foundation.
Real lives changed.

Despite being in its early stages, The N. Johnson Foundation has already made meaningful, measurable contributions to students and communities in Belize. Every number below represents a child who showed up to school this year because someone believed in them.

Where We Serve

Our heart is
in Belize —
and growing.

The Foundation was born from a deep personal connection to Belizean communities where educational access and financial hardship prevent talented, eager children from reaching their potential. We show up with culturally respectful, community-first solutions — not charity, but solidarity.

From our base in Torrance, California, we raise funds, build partnerships, and coordinate programs that flow directly to the students and schools that need them most.

Support Belize
Nicole Johnson, MHA Founder & Executive Director
Monica Stuart, Attorney-at-Law · Co-Founder
Our Founders

Led by lived experience.
Driven by purpose.

Nicole Johnson is a U.S. Veteran, published author, and Medi-Cal Certified Peer Support Specialist with a Master of Healthcare Administration. Her years in behavioral health — combined with her own lived experience — give her a rare ability to bridge systems thinking with deep human compassion.

Monica Stuart, Attorney-at-Law and Co-Founder, brings governance, compliance, and advocacy expertise — ensuring the Foundation operates with transparency, accountability, and an unwavering ethical foundation.

MHA · Master of Healthcare Administration CPSS Certified U.S. Veteran Published Author Attorney-at-Law
More about our team
Get Involved

Help a child focus on
learning, not hardship.

Your donation directly funds scholarships, school supplies, and community programs that promote both learning and emotional well-being. Every contribution — large or small — means a child shows up to school with what they need.

Donate now Buy a book →
$25 — School supplies for one child A backpack, notebook, and pens so a student walks in prepared and proud.
$100 — Scholarship contribution Helps cover fees so a family isn't forced to pull their child from school.
$250 — Classroom resource bundle Books, materials & tools donated directly to a school in Belize.
